terms.green_wave: an unbroken wave face rather than whitewater
terms.trim: maintaining an efficient line and speed along the wave
terms.bottom_turn: the foundational turn from the lower face back toward the pocket
terms.cutback: a turn back toward the breaking part of the wave
terms.rail_line: the effective curved edge engaged through the water
terms.planing_area: surface area helping the board lift and travel across the water
terms.wide_point: the widest part of the outline and its position relative to centre
terms.rocker: the board's longitudinal curve from nose to tail
terms.concave: inward bottom contour that influences lift and water flow
terms.vee: outward bottom angle helping rail-to-rail transition
terms.glide: ability to maintain momentum with limited active input
terms.projection: drive carried forward through a turn
terms.release: ease with which fins or tail free up and redirect
terms.hold: security and grip through steep or powerful sections
terms.forgiveness: tolerance of imperfect timing, stance or pressure
terms.duck_dive: submerging a board under an approaching wave
terms.step_up: a longer, more controlled board for larger or more powerful waves
terms.groveller: a compact board optimised to generate speed in weak surf
